title: A repeated awakening study exploring the capacity of complexity measures to capture dreaming during propofol sedation
description: This dataset contains electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from a repeated awakening study investigating the effects of propofol sedation on dreaming and the applicability of complexity measures in capturing consciousness dynamics. The study examines EEG complexity measures as potential biomarkers for subjective experiences during pharmacological sedation, with recordings obtained during wakefulness, sedation, and repeated awakenings. Data were collected from multiple subjects across multiple sessions and organized according to BIDS standards.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during multiple conditions including eyes-closed and eyes-open wakefulness, propofol sedation with resting recordings, and transcranial magnetic stimulation sessions. Data were recorded using BrainVision equipment and organized according to BIDS standards with multiple runs capturing different awakening episodes during sedation.

funding_references.funder_name: Regional Committees for Medical Research Ethics South East Norway (REK)
